evals.prompt: Test my Power Pages site at https://contoso-portal.powerappsportals.com and make sure all the pages and APIs are working correctly.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the provided URL directly without needing to resolve it from activation status. It launches a Playwright browser, resizes the viewport, navigates to the homepage, and verifies it loads successfully. It captures console errors and network requests during the initial load. Since the site is public (no login wall), it proceeds to crawl discoverable links up to a maximum of 25 pages, checking each for errors. It captures /_api/ requests, analyzes their status codes (all returning 200 OK), and confirms the APIs are healthy. Finally, it generates a test report with pass/fail results per page and per API endpoint, closes the browser, and provides next steps.
expectations: Uses the user-provided URL https://contoso-portal.powerappsportals.com directly without prompting or checking activation status
Resizes the browser viewport to an appropriate dimension before navigating
Navigates to the homepage URL and verifies it loads without critical errors
Captures and reports any browser console errors during page loads
Captures network requests during navigation to identify API calls
Detects no login wall and proceeds without asking the user to authenticate
Crawls discoverable links on the site, visiting up to a maximum of 25 pages
Checks each crawled page for load errors, console errors, and failed network requests
Identifies /_api/ requests and analyzes their HTTP status codes
Reports all API endpoints as healthy when status codes are 200
Generates a final test report with per-page pass/fail results and per-API-endpoint results
Closes the browser session after testing is complete
Provides actionable next steps or recommendations based on the test results

evals.prompt: Run a full test on my Power Pages site. I don't have the URL handy but it should be activated already.
evals.expected_output: The agent detects that no URL was provided. It attempts to auto-detect the site URL by checking the activation status of the Power Pages site in the current environment. Once the URL is resolved from the activation status, it launches a Playwright browser, resizes the viewport, navigates to the resolved homepage, and verifies it loads. It captures console errors and network requests, confirms no login wall is present, crawls up to 25 pages, tests API endpoints, and generates a comprehensive test report before closing the browser.
expectations: Recognizes that the user did not provide a site URL
Attempts to resolve the site URL from the activation status or environment configuration
Falls back to asking the user for the URL if auto-detection fails
Resizes the browser viewport before navigating to the site
Navigates to the resolved homepage and verifies successful load
Captures browser console errors and network requests during navigation
Crawls discoverable links up to a maximum of 25 pages
Identifies and analyzes /_api/ network requests with their status codes
Generates a test report summarizing pass/fail results for each page and API endpoint
Closes the browser after all tests are complete
Provides recommendations or next steps based on test findings

evals.prompt: Test https://hr-portal.powerappsportals.com to see if all pages load correctly. This site requires employees to log in.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the provided URL, launches a Playwright browser, resizes the viewport, and navigates to the homepage. During the initial load, it detects a login wall (redirect to a sign-in page or presence of authentication prompts). It informs the user that authentication is required and asks the user to complete the login process in the browser. After the user logs in, the agent resumes testing by verifying the authenticated homepage, capturing console errors and network requests, crawling up to 25 pages behind the login wall, testing API endpoints, and generating a full test report before closing the browser.
expectations: Uses the user-provided URL https://hr-portal.powerappsportals.com directly
Resizes the browser viewport to an appropriate dimension
Navigates to the homepage and detects a login wall or authentication redirect
Informs the user that the site requires authentication and asks them to log in
Waits for the user to complete the login process before continuing
After authentication, verifies the homepage loads correctly in the authenticated state
Captures browser console errors and network requests throughout testing
Crawls discoverable links up to a maximum of 25 pages in the authenticated context
Identifies /_api/ requests and analyzes their HTTP status codes
Generates a test report with per-page and per-API pass/fail results
Closes the browser session after testing is complete
Provides next steps or recommendations based on the test outcomes

evals.prompt: I need to test https://partner-portal.powerappsportals.com -- some of our API endpoints have been returning errors lately. Please check everything.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the provided URL, launches a Playwright browser, resizes the viewport, and navigates to the homepage. It verifies the homepage loads, captures console errors and network requests, and confirms no login wall is present. It crawls up to 25 pages, finding some pages that load correctly and others that may show errors. Critically, it captures /_api/ requests and identifies several returning 403 Forbidden status codes. It provides detailed remediation guidance for the 403 errors, such as checking table permissions, web roles, and site settings. The final test report includes per-page pass/fail results, per-API-endpoint results with status codes, specific remediation steps for failing endpoints, and an overall summary with recommendations.
expectations: Uses the user-provided URL https://partner-portal.powerappsportals.com directly
Resizes the browser viewport before navigating to the site
Navigates to the homepage and verifies it loads without critical failure
Captures browser console errors during all page loads
Captures network requests including /_api/ calls during navigation
Detects no login wall and proceeds with testing without authentication prompts
Crawls discoverable links on the site up to a maximum of 25 pages
Checks each crawled page for load errors and console issues
Identifies /_api/ requests that return 403 Forbidden status codes
Provides actionable remediation guidance for 403 errors such as reviewing table permissions, web roles, or site settings
Generates a final test report with per-page pass/fail results and per-API-endpoint results including status codes
Includes an overall summary distinguishing healthy endpoints from failing ones
Closes the browser session after all testing is complete
Provides prioritized next steps focusing on resolving the API failures

evals.prompt: Test my Power Pages site at https://intranet.powerappsportals.com. It uses SAML2 authentication with our corporate ADFS server, so you'll need to let me log in first.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the provided URL, launches a Playwright browser, resizes the viewport, and navigates to the homepage. It detects a redirect to the ADFS login page (a SAML2 identity provider on a different domain). It informs the user that the site redirected to a SAML2/ADFS login page and asks the user to complete authentication in the browser window. After the user logs in through the ADFS portal and is redirected back to the Power Pages site, the agent verifies the homepage loaded with authenticated content. It then crawls up to 25 pages, captures API requests, and generates a test report.
expectations: Uses the user-provided URL https://intranet.powerappsportals.com directly
Resizes the browser viewport to an appropriate dimension
Navigates to the homepage and detects a redirect to a SAML2/ADFS identity provider login page
Recognizes the ADFS login page as a private site gate authentication redirect
Informs the user that the site requires SAML2/ADFS authentication and asks them to log in
Waits for the user to complete the ADFS login process before continuing
After authentication, verifies the homepage loads correctly with authenticated content
Captures browser console errors and network requests throughout testing
Crawls discoverable links up to a maximum of 25 pages in the authenticated context
Identifies /_api/ requests and analyzes their HTTP status codes
Generates a test report with per-page and per-API pass/fail results
Closes the browser session after testing is complete
Provides next steps or recommendations based on the test outcomes

evals.prompt: Test https://self-service.powerappsportals.com. This site uses local authentication with username/password login. I'll create a test account and log in.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the provided URL, launches a Playwright browser, resizes the viewport, and navigates to the homepage. The homepage loads successfully as a public site. The agent detects site-level authentication indicators (a Sign in or Log in link in the navigation) and recognizes the site supports local authentication (login form with username/password fields rather than external provider redirect). It asks the user to log in through the browser's login form. After the user authenticates with their local credentials, the agent proceeds to crawl pages and test APIs, generating a comprehensive report.
expectations: Uses the user-provided URL https://self-service.powerappsportals.com directly
Resizes the browser viewport before navigating to the site
Navigates to the homepage and verifies it loads without critical errors
Detects site-level authentication indicators such as Sign in or Log in links
Recognizes local authentication when the login page shows username/email and password fields instead of redirecting to an external identity provider
Asks the user to complete the local login through the browser window
After authentication, verifies the user is now logged in
Crawls discoverable links up to a maximum of 25 pages
Captures and analyzes /_api/ network requests
Generates a test report with per-page and per-API pass/fail results
Closes the browser session after testing is complete
Provides recommendations based on test findings

evals.prompt: Run tests on https://partner-hub.powerappsportals.com. The site uses OpenID Connect with Okta for authentication. Some pages are public and some require login.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the provided URL, launches a Playwright browser, and navigates to the homepage. The homepage loads as a public site with a Sign in option visible. The agent detects site-level authentication and asks whether the user wants to log in or skip authenticated pages. If the user logs in through the Okta OIDC flow (redirect to Okta login page, then back to the site), the agent tests both public and authenticated pages. The report distinguishes between public and authenticated page results and notes any APIs returning 401/403 for unauthenticated vs authenticated contexts.
expectations: Uses the user-provided URL https://partner-hub.powerappsportals.com directly
Resizes the browser viewport to an appropriate dimension
Navigates to the homepage and verifies it loads correctly as a public page
Detects site-level authentication indicators (Sign in link)
Asks user whether to log in or skip authenticated pages
If user logs in, recognizes the redirect to Okta as an OpenID Connect authentication flow
Waits for the user to complete the Okta login process
After authentication, verifies the user is logged in on the site
Crawls discoverable links up to a maximum of 25 pages
Captures and analyzes /_api/ network requests with status codes
Generates a test report distinguishing public and authenticated page results
Closes the browser session after testing is complete
Provides recommendations based on test findings
